title: Anatomy of an Embargo Breach
text:
Welcome to our ISSS608 Visual Analytics project.
This site documents our team’s work building an interactive Shiny application that investigates how a corporate merger embargo was broken by a team of AI communication agents. Use the navigation bar to move through our proposal, methodology, findings, and the application itself.
Our challenge is based on the VAST Challenge 2026 Mini-Challenge 1.

title: Meeting Minutes
text: A running record of every team meeting. Each entry notes who attended, what was discussed, decisions made, and the action items agreed with an owner for each.

title: Project Proposal
text:
Motivation
State the real-world problem that motivates this project. Avoid saying the project was assigned. Frame it around the embargo breach scenario and why an investigator would need a tool to understand what happened.


Objectives
List what the application will let a user do. For example, trace the sequence of a breach, compare normal behaviour to a crisis, and surface earlier warning signs, all through interactive controls rather than fixed charts.


Data
Describe the VAST Challenge 2026 Mini-Challenge 1 dataset. Number of rounds, messages, agents, the time span, and the structure of the JSON.


Methodology
Explain how the data flows from raw JSON to interactive app. A flow diagram goes here. The three methodology pages cover preparation, analysis, and visualisation in detail.


Prototype and Storyboard
Sketch the planned Shiny app layout. Show the main inputs the user controls and the outputs that update. Hand sketches or simple wireframes are fine.


R Packages
List the packages the project will use, grouped by purpose such as utility, plotting, network analysis, and Shiny.


Project Schedule
A Gantt chart of the timeline from proposal to final submission goes here.
